tu.prop: The principle idea of our approach is to find the dependence relations between variables that represent different linguistic units (word class concept...) that constitutes a language model.

tuv.seg: Language Modeling using Dynamic Bayesian Networks In this paper we propose a new approach to language modeling based on dynamic Bayesian networks.
Modélisation du langage à l'aide de réseaux bayésiens dynamiques Dans cet article, nous proposons une nouvelle approche pour la modélisation du langage basée sur des réseaux bayésiens dynamiques.

tu.prop: Language Modeling using Dynamic Bayesian Networks In this paper we propose a new approach to language modeling based on dynamic Bayesian networks.
In the context of this paper the linguistic units that we consider are syntactic classes and words.

tuv.seg: The principle idea of our approach is to find the dependence relations between variables that represent different linguistic units (word class concept...) that constitutes a language model.

L'idée principale de notre approche est de trouver les relations de dépendance entre les variables qui représentent différentes unités linguistiques (concept de classe de mots...) qui constituent un modèle de langage.

tu.prop: The principle idea of our approach is to find the dependence relations between variables that represent different linguistic units (word class concept...) that constitutes a language model.
Our approach should not be considered as a model combination technique.

tuv.seg: In the context of this paper the linguistic units that we consider are syntactic classes and words.
Dans le cadre de cet article, les unités linguistiques que nous considérons sont des classes syntaxiques et des mots.

tu.prop: In the context of this paper the linguistic units that we consider are syntactic classes and words.
Rather it is an original and coherent methodology that processes words and classes in the same model.

tuv.seg: Our approach should not be considered as a model combination technique.
Notre approche ne doit pas être considérée comme une technique de combinaison de modèles.

tu.prop: Our approach should not be considered as a model combination technique.
We attempt to identify and model the dependence of words and classes on their linguistic context.

tuv.seg: Rather it is an original and coherent methodology that processes words and classes in the same model.
Il s'agit plutôt d'une méthodologie originale et cohérente qui traite les mots et les classes dans un même modèle.

tu.prop: Rather it is an original and coherent methodology that processes words and classes in the same model.
Our ultimate goal is to devise an automatic mechanism that extracts the best dependence relations between a word and its context i.e. lexical and syntactic.

tuv.seg: We attempt to identify and model the dependence of words and classes on their linguistic context.
Nous essayons d'identifier et de modéliser la dépendance des mots et des classes à leur contexte linguistique.

tu.prop: We attempt to identify and model the dependence of words and classes on their linguistic context.
Preliminary results are very encouraging in particular the model in which a word depends not only on previous word but also on syntactic classes of two previous words.

tuv.seg: Our ultimate goal is to devise an automatic mechanism that extracts the best dependence relations between a word and its context i.e. lexical and syntactic.
Notre but ultime est de concevoir un mécanisme automatique qui extrait les meilleures relations de dépendance entre un mot et son contexte, c'est-à-dire lexical et syntaxique.

tu.prop: Our ultimate goal is to devise an automatic mechanism that extracts the best dependence relations between a word and its context i.e. lexical and syntactic.
This model outperforms the bi-gram model.

tuv.seg: Preliminary results are very encouraging in particular the model in which a word depends not only on previous word but also on syntactic classes of two previous words.
Les résultats préliminaires sont très encourageants, en particulier le modèle dans lequel un mot dépend non seulement du mot précédent mais aussi des classes syntaxiques de deux mots précédents.

tu.prop: Preliminary results are very encouraging in particular the model in which a word depends not only on previous word but also on syntactic classes of two previous words.

tuv.seg: This model outperforms the bi-gram model.
Ce modèle surpasse le modèle bi-gramme.

tu.prop: This model outperforms the bi-gram model.
The role of a statistical language model is to model different linguistic events in a language.

tuv.seg: Introduction
Introduction

tu.prop: This is achieved by assigning a probability to each word sequence hw t (where h represent the history of word w t ).

tuv.seg: The role of a statistical language model is to model different linguistic events in a language.
Le rôle d'un modèle de langage statistique est de modéliser les différents événements langagiers de la langue.

tu.prop: The role of a statistical language model is to model different linguistic events in a language.
These probabilities are estimated using a Markov chain of some fixed order ( usually 1 or 2 ).

tuv.seg: This is achieved by assigning a probability to each word sequence hw t (where h represent the history of word w t ).
Cela est réalisé en affectant une probabilité à chaque séquence de mots hw t (où h désigne l'historique du mot w t ).

tu.prop: This is achieved by assigning a probability to each word sequence hw t (where h represent the history of word w t ).
n-grams formulated as Markov chains are the most widely used language models.

tuv.seg: These probabilities are estimated using a Markov chain of some fixed order ( usually 1 or 2 ).
Cette probabilité est estimée à l'aide d'une chaîne de Markov d'un certain ordre (souvent 1 ou 2).

tu.prop: These probabilities are estimated using a Markov chain of some fixed order ( usually 1 or 2 ).
Based on the observation that certain words have similar behavior one can think of clustering words into classes and construct n-class models to substitute or complement n-grams.

tuv.seg: n-grams formulated as Markov chains are the most widely used language models.
Les n-grammes formulés comme des chaînes de Markov sont les modèles de langage les plus couramment utilisés.

tu.prop: n-grams formulated as Markov chains are the most widely used language models.
This approach is used to reduce model complexity improve generalization property handle missing data and introduce high level linguistic information (syntactic semantic etc.).

tuv.seg: Based on the observation that certain words have similar behavior one can think of clustering words into classes and construct n-class models to substitute or complement n-grams.
En partant du constat que certains mots ont un comportement similaire, on peut regrouper les mots en classes et construire des modèles dits n-classes pour remplacer ou compléter les n-grammes.

tu.prop: Based on the observation that certain words have similar behavior one can think of clustering words into classes and construct n-class models to substitute or complement n-grams.
Different complementary linguistic information sources can be exploited using simple linear ombination (Jelinek and Mercer 1980) or maximum entropy (Rosenfeld 1994) techniques.

tuv.seg: This approach is used to reduce model complexity improve generalization property handle missing data and introduce high level linguistic information (syntactic semantic etc.).

Cette approche est utilisée pour réduire la complexité du modèle, améliorer la propriété de généralisation, gérer les données manquantes et introduire des informations linguistiques de haut niveau (syntaxiques, sémantiques, etc.).

tu.prop: This approach is used to reduce model complexity improve generalization property handle missing data and introduce high level linguistic information (syntactic semantic etc.).
As it will become clear in the following sections ngram and n-class models are actually particular cases of dynamic Bayesian networks (DBN).

tuv.seg: Different complementary linguistic information sources can be exploited using simple linear ombination (Jelinek and Mercer 1980) or maximum entropy (Rosenfeld 1994) techniques.

L'exploitation de différentes sources d'informations linguistiques complémentaires peut se faire par une simple combinaison linéaire (Jelinek et Mercer, 1980) ou par l'utilisation du maximum d'entropie (Rosenfeld, 1994).

tu.prop: Different complementary linguistic information sources can be exploited using simple linear ombination (Jelinek and Mercer 1980) or maximum entropy (Rosenfeld 1994) techniques.
In this paper we propose to use the DBN formalism in order to achieve a better exploitation of each linguistic unit considered in modeling.

tuv.seg: As it will become clear in the following sections ngram and n-class models are actually particular cases of dynamic Bayesian networks (DBN).
Comme nous le verrons dans les sections suivantes, les modèles n-grammes et n-classes sont en fait des cas particuliers de réseaux Bayésiens dynamiques (RBD).

tu.prop: As it will become clear in the following sections ngram and n-class models are actually particular cases of dynamic Bayesian networks (DBN).
We develop a unifying approach that processes each of these units in one model and construct new language models with improved performance.

tuv.seg: In this paper we propose to use the DBN formalism in order to achieve a better exploitation of each linguistic unit considered in modeling.
Dans cet article, nous proposons d'utiliser le formalisme des RBD afin de mieux exploiter chacune des unités linguistiques modélisées.

tu.prop: In this paper we propose to use the DBN formalism in order to achieve a better exploitation of each linguistic unit considered in modeling.
The principle of our approach is to construct DBNs in which a variable (word class or any other linguistic unit) may depend on a set of context variables.

tuv.seg: We develop a unifying approach that processes each of these units in one model and construct new language models with improved performance.
Nous développons une approche unifiée qui traite chacune de ces unités dans un même modèle et construit de nouveaux modèles de langage avec des performances améliorées.

tu.prop: We develop a unifying approach that processes each of these units in one model and construct new language models with improved performance.
These dependence links between linguistic units can be determined automatically or manually.

tuv.seg: The principle of our approach is to construct DBNs in which a variable (word class or any other linguistic unit) may depend on a set of context variables.
Le principe de cette approche est de construire des RBD où une variable (qui peut être un mot ou une classe ou toute autre unité linguistique) peut dépendre d'un sous-ensemble de variables.

tu.prop: The principle of our approach is to construct DBNs in which a variable (word class or any other linguistic unit) may depend on a set of context variables.
Our ultimate goal is to propose an automatic scheme to learn the optimal DBN structure from a training corpus.

tuv.seg: These dependence links between linguistic units can be determined automatically or manually.
Ces liens de dépendance entre unités linguistiques peuvent être déterminés automatiquement ou manuellement.

tu.prop: These dependence links between linguistic units can be determined automatically or manually.
With this goal in mind in this paper we investigate the feasibility of our approach by first testing models for which the structure is specified manually.

tuv.seg: Our ultimate goal is to propose an automatic scheme to learn the optimal DBN structure from a training corpus.
Notre objectif ultime est de proposer un processus automatique pour apprendre la structure du RBD optimal à partir d'un corpus d'apprentissage.

tu.prop: Our ultimate goal is to propose an automatic scheme to learn the optimal DBN structure from a training corpus.

The advantage of our approach with respect to linear interpolation is that instead of using a weighted average of models constructed over words and classes separately our approach integrates all linguistic units in one model without any distinction between classes and words.

tuv.seg: With this goal in mind in this paper we investigate the feasibility of our approach by first testing models for which the structure is specified manually.
Dans le but d'atteindre cet objectif, nous avons dans cet article étudié la faisabilité de notre approche en testant d'abord des modèles pour lesquels la structure est spécifiée manuellement.

tu.prop: With this goal in mind in this paper we investigate the feasibility of our approach by first testing models for which the structure is specified manually.
Compared to the maximum entropy principle we can say that our approach yields models that are much easier to interpret.

tuv.seg: The advantage of our approach with respect to linear interpolation is that instead of using a weighted average of models constructed over words and classes separately our approach integrates all linguistic units in one model without any distinction between classes and words.

Soulignons qu'un avantage de notre approche par rapport à l'interpolation linéaire est qu'elle intègre toutes les unités linguistiques au sein d'une même structure, sans distinction entre classes et mots, au lieu d'utiliser une moyenne pondérée entre des modèles construits séparément sur les mots et les classes.

tu.prop: The advantage of our approach with respect to linear interpolation is that instead of using a weighted average of models constructed over words and classes separately our approach integrates all linguistic units in one model without any distinction between classes and words.

tuv.seg: Compared to the maximum entropy principle we can say that our approach yields models that are much easier to interpret.
Comparé au principe du maximum d'entropie on peut dire que notre approche conduit à des modèles qui sont bien plus simples à interpréter.

tu.prop: Compared to the maximum entropy principle we can say that our approach yields models that are much easier to interpret.
Our approach is based on the framework of dynamic Bayesian networks (DBNs) which is a generalization of Bayesian networks (BNs) to dynamic processes.

tuv.seg: Dynamic Bayesian networks
Réseaux bayésiens dynamiques

tu.prop: Briefly the Bayesian networks formalism consists of associating a directed acyclic graph to the joint probability distribution (JPD) P (X) of a set of random variables X = {X 1...

tuv.seg: Our approach is based on the framework of dynamic Bayesian networks (DBNs) which is a generalization of Bayesian networks (BNs) to dynamic processes.
Notre approche est fondée sur le formalisme des réseaux bayésiens dynamiques (RBD), qui est une généralisation des réseaux Bayésiens aux processus dynamiques.

tu.prop: Our approach is based on the framework of dynamic Bayesian networks (DBNs) which is a generalization of Bayesian networks (BNs) to dynamic processes.

tuv.seg: Briefly the Bayesian networks formalism consists of associating a directed acyclic graph to the joint probability distribution (JPD) P (X) of a set of random variables X = {X 1...

Brièvement, le formalisme des réseaux bayésiens consiste à associer un graphe orienté acyclique à la distribution jointe des probabilités (DCP) P (X) d'un ensemble de variables aléatoires X = {X 1... X n }.

tu.prop: Briefly the Bayesian networks formalism consists of associating a directed acyclic graph to the joint probability distribution (JPD) P (X) of a set of random variables X = {X 1...
The nodes of this graph represent the random variables while the arrows encode the conditional independences (CI) which (are supposed to) exist in the JPD.

tuv.seg: X n }.

tu.prop: A BN is completely defined by a graph structure S and the numerical parametrization Θ of the conditional probabilities of the variables given their parents.

tuv.seg: The nodes of this graph represent the random variables while the arrows encode the conditional independences (CI) which (are supposed to) exist in the JPD.
Les noeuds de ce graphe représentent les variables aléatoires et les arcs codent les indépendances conditionnelles (IC) qui existent (en théorie) dans la DCP.

tu.prop: The nodes of this graph represent the random variables while the arrows encode the conditional independences (CI) which (are supposed to) exist in the JPD.
Indeed the JPD can be expressed in a factored way as P (X) = n i=1 P (X i |Π i ) where Π i denotes the parents of X i in S.

tuv.seg: A BN is completely defined by a graph structure S and the numerical parametrization Θ of the conditional probabilities of the variables given their parents.
Un réseau bayésien est complètement défini par une structure graphique S et une paramétrisation numérique Θ des probabilités conditionnelles des variables sachant leurs parents.

tu.prop: A BN is completely defined by a graph structure S and the numerical parametrization Θ of the conditional probabilities of the variables given their parents.
A DBN encodes the joint probability distribution of a time evolving set

tuv.seg: Indeed the JPD can be expressed in a factored way as P (X) = n i=1 P (X i |Π i ) where Π i denotes the parents of X i in S.
En effet, la DCP peut se factoriser sous la forme P(X) = n i=1 P(Xi |Πi) où Πi dénote les parents de Xi dans S.

tu.prop: Indeed the JPD can be expressed in a factored way as P (X) = n i=1 P (X i |Π i ) where Π i denotes the parents of X i in S.
If we consider T time slices of variables the DBN can be considered as a (static) BN with T × n variables.

tuv.seg: A DBN encodes the joint probability distribution of a time evolving set
Un RBD code la distribution de probabilité jointe d'un ensemble évoluant dans le temps.

tu.prop: A DBN encodes the joint probability distribution of a time evolving set
Using the factorization property of BNs the joint probability density of X T 1 = {X...

tuv.seg: If we consider T time slices of variables the DBN can be considered as a (static) BN with T × n variables.

tu.prop: If we consider T time slices of variables the DBN can be considered as a (static) BN with T × n variables.

tuv.seg: Using the factorization property of BNs the joint probability density of X T 1 = {X...

tu.prop: Using the factorization property of BNs the joint probability density of X T 1 = {X...
where Π it denotes the parents of X i [t].

tuv.seg: X[T ]} is written as:
X[T ]} s'écrit comme :

tu.prop: In the BNs literature DBNs are defined using the assumption that X[t] is Markovian (Friedman et al.

tuv.seg: where Π it denotes the parents of X i [t].
où Π it dénote les parents de X i [t].

tu.prop: where Π it denotes the parents of X i [t].

tuv.seg: In the BNs literature DBNs are defined using the assumption that X[t] is Markovian (Friedman et al.
Dans la littérature, les RBD sont définis en faisant l'hypothèse que X[t] est un processus markovien (Friedman et al., 1998).

tu.prop: In the BNs literature DBNs are defined using the assumption that X[t] is Markovian (Friedman et al.
In this paper we relax this hypothesis to allow non-Markov processes and we consider that the process X[t] satisfies:
for some integers τ p and τ f.

tuv.seg: In this paper we relax this hypothesis to allow non-Markov processes and we consider that the process X[t] satisfies:
Dans cet article, nous affaiblissons cette hypothèse pour autoriser des processus non-markoviens et pour que le processus X[t] satisfasse :

tu.prop: In this paper we relax this hypothesis to allow non-Markov processes and we consider that the process X[t] satisfies:
Graphically the above assumption states that a variable at time t can have parents in the interval [tτ p t + τ f ] (see (Deviren and Daoudi 2001) for details).

tuv.seg: for some integers τ p and τ f.
) pour des entiers τ p et τ f.

tu.prop: for some integers τ p and τ f.
From this perspective it is obvious that classical language models can be represented as DBNs.

tuv.seg: Graphically the above assumption states that a variable at time t can have parents in the interval [tτ p t + τ f ] (see (Deviren and Daoudi 2001) for details).
Graphiquement, l'hypothèse ci-dessus signifie qu'une variable au temps t peut avoir des parents dans l'intervalle [tτ p t + τ f ] (voir (Deviren et Daoudi 2001) pour plus de détails).

tu.prop: Graphically the above assumption states that a variable at time t can have parents in the interval [tτ p t + τ f ] (see (Deviren and Daoudi 2001) for details).
Indeed n-gram models assume that the probability of a word sequence is factorized over the conditional probabilities of each word in the sequence given its recent history of n-1 words.

tuv.seg: From this perspective it is obvious that classical language models can be represented as DBNs.
De ce point de vue il est clair que les modèles de langage classiques peuvent être représentés comme des RBD.

tu.prop: From this perspective it is obvious that classical language models can be represented as DBNs.
That is if W is the word vocabulary and w T 1 = w 1...w T ∈ W T is a word sequence one assumes that:

tuv.seg: Indeed n-gram models assume that the probability of a word sequence is factorized over the conditional probabilities of each word in the sequence given its recent history of n-1 words.

En effet, les modèles n-grammes supposent que la probabilité d'une séquence de mots se factorise comme le produit des probabilités conditionnelles de chaque mot sachant son historique récent de n-1 mots.

tu.prop: Indeed n-gram models assume that the probability of a word sequence is factorized over the conditional probabilities of each word in the sequence given its recent history of n-1 words.
Thus if W t is a discrete random variable taking its values in W for every t n-grams can be represented as the DBN shown in Fig. 1

tuv.seg: That is if W is the word vocabulary and w T 1 = w 1...w T ∈ W T is a word sequence one assumes that:
Plus précisément, si V est l'ensemble des mots et w T 1 = w 1...w T ∈ V T est une séquence on suppose que :

tu.prop: That is if W is the word vocabulary and w T 1 = w 1...w T ∈ W T is a word sequence one assumes that:

tuv.seg: Thus if W t is a discrete random variable taking its values in W for every t n-grams can be represented as the DBN shown in Fig. 1
Ainsi, si W t est une variable aléatoire discrète prenant ses valeurs dans W, pour chaque valeur de t, les n-grammes peuvent être représentés sous la forme du RBD présenté sur la figure 1.

tu.prop: Thus if W t is a discrete random variable taking its values in W for every t n-grams can be represented as the DBN shown in Fig. 1
n-gram and n-class models are the most commonly used language models in state-of-the-art speech recognition systems.

tuv.seg: DBNs for language modeling
RBD pour la modélisation linguistique

tu.prop: It has been shown that n-gram models are very efficient when they are trained on a sufficiently informative corpus.

tuv.seg: n-gram and n-class models are the most commonly used language models in state-of-the-art speech recognition systems.
Les modèles n-grammes et n-classes sont les modèles de langage les plus couramment utilisés dans les systèmes de reconnaissance vocale de l'état de l'art.

tu.prop: n-gram and n-class models are the most commonly used language models in state-of-the-art speech recognition systems.
The principle inconvenience of these models is that they are computationally very demanding given that they require |W | n probabilities to be estimated where n is the model order.

tuv.seg: It has been shown that n-gram models are very efficient when they are trained on a sufficiently informative corpus.
Il a été démontré que les modèles n-grammes sont très efficaces lorsqu'ils sont entrainés à partir d'un corpus suffisamment informatif.

tu.prop: It has been shown that n-gram models are very efficient when they are trained on a sufficiently informative corpus.
Despite the "cut-off" techniques that reduce the complexity these models remain extremely complex.

tuv.seg: The principle inconvenience of these models is that they are computationally very demanding given that they require |W | n probabilities to be estimated where n is the model order.
L'inconvénient principal de ces modèles est qu'ils sont très exigeants en termes computationnels, car ils nécessitent l'estimation de |W | n probabilités, où n est l'ordre du modèle.

tu.prop: The principle inconvenience of these models is that they are computationally very demanding given that they require |W | n probabilities to be estimated where n is the model order.
On the other hand n-class models are less complex since the number of classes is generally much smaller than the vocabulary size.

tuv.seg: Despite the "cut-off" techniques that reduce the complexity these models remain extremely complex.
Même si les techniques de « cut-off » en réduisent la complexité, ces modèles restent extrêmement complexes.

tu.prop: Despite the "cut-off" techniques that reduce the complexity these models remain extremely complex.
They also have better generalization properties.

tuv.seg: On the other hand n-class models are less complex since the number of classes is generally much smaller than the vocabulary size.
D'un autre côté, les modèles de classe n sont moins complexes, puisque le nombre de classes est généralement beaucoup plus petit que la taille du vocabulaire.

tu.prop: On the other hand n-class models are less complex since the number of classes is generally much smaller than the vocabulary size.
Nevertheless they are less accurate and their perplexity is higher.

tuv.seg: They also have better generalization properties.
Ils présentent également de meilleures propriétés de généralisation.

tu.prop: They also have better generalization properties.
For this reason they are generally combined with n-grams.

tuv.seg: Nevertheless they are less accurate and their perplexity is higher.
Cependant, ils sont moins précis et leur perplexité est plus élevée.

tu.prop: Nevertheless they are less accurate and their perplexity is higher.

In such circumstances the realization of a language model is performed first by specifying the model order (n) and then the two models ( n-gram and nclass) are trained individually using maximum likelihood criteria.

tuv.seg: For this reason they are generally combined with n-grams.
Pour cette raison, ils sont généralement combinés avec des n-grammes.

tu.prop: For this reason they are generally combined with n-grams.
The last step is either a linear combination of these models or an integration of their respective characteristics in a single architecture using maximum entropy techniques.

tuv.seg: In such circumstances the realization of a language model is performed first by specifying the model order (n) and then the two models ( n-gram and nclass) are trained individually using maximum likelihood criteria.

Dans ces circonstances, la réalisation d'un modèle de langage est effectuée en spécifiant l'ordre du modèle (n) et ensuite les deux modèles (n-grammes et n-classes) sont entraînés séparément en utilisant le critère du maximum de vraisemblance.

tu.prop: In such circumstances the realization of a language model is performed first by specifying the model order (n) and then the two models ( n-gram and nclass) are trained individually using maximum likelihood criteria.

This approach yields quite interesting results however if we want to better exploit the lexical and syntactic information a solution would be to consider them in a unique model that is trained within a single procedure.

tuv.seg: The last step is either a linear combination of these models or an integration of their respective characteristics in a single architecture using maximum entropy techniques.
La dernière étape est une combinaison linéaire de ces modèles ou une intégration de leurs caractéristiques respectives dans une architecture unique à l'aide de techniques du maximum d'entropie.

tu.prop: The last step is either a linear combination of these models or an integration of their respective characteristics in a single architecture using maximum entropy techniques.
The DBN formalism provides a theoretical and computational framework to achieve our goal.

tuv.seg: This approach yields quite interesting results however if we want to better exploit the lexical and syntactic information a solution would be to consider them in a unique model that is trained within a single procedure.

Cette approche donne des résultats intéressants mais si l'on veut mieux exploiter les informations lexicales et syntaxiques il faudrait les intégrer dans un modèle unique qui est entrainé en une seule étape.

tu.prop: This approach yields quite interesting results however if we want to better exploit the lexical and syntactic information a solution would be to consider them in a unique model that is trained within a single procedure.

Our principle idea is to impose no a priori hypothesis on the way a language should be represented but to consider all available data (words classes...) as observations of the dynamic system {W t C t }.

tuv.seg: The DBN formalism provides a theoretical and computational framework to achieve our goal.
Le formalisme des RBD nous offre un cadre théorique et algorithmique pour réaliser cet objectif.

tu.prop: The DBN formalism provides a theoretical and computational framework to achieve our goal.
Our goal then is to find the model that has the best description (in terms of perplexity) of these observations.

tuv.seg: Our principle idea is to impose no a priori hypothesis on the way a language should be represented but to consider all available data (words classes...) as observations of the dynamic system {W t C t }.

Notre idée principale est de ne faire aucune hypothèse a priori sur la façon dont une langue doit être représentée mais de considérer toutes les données disponibles (classes de mots...) comme des observations du système dynamique {W t C t }.

tu.prop: Our principle idea is to impose no a priori hypothesis on the way a language should be represented but to consider all available data (words classes...) as observations of the dynamic system {W t C t }.
In this way we let data dictate what influences the pronunciation of a word.

tuv.seg: Our goal then is to find the model that has the best description (in terms of perplexity) of these observations.
Notre objectif est alors de trouver le modèle qui fournit la meilleure description (en termes de perplexité) de ces observations.

tu.prop: Our goal then is to find the model that has the best description (in terms of perplexity) of these observations.
In Bayesian networks terminology this is the structure learning problem: find the graph structure (and its numerical parameterization) that explains the data at ''best".

tuv.seg: In this way we let data dictate what influences the pronunciation of a word.
De cette façon nous laissons les données dicter ce qui influence la prononciation d'un mot.

tu.prop: In this way we let data dictate what influences the pronunciation of a word.
In BN literature there are algorithms that attempt to solve this (difficult) problem (Heckerman 1995) their performances depend on the application.

tuv.seg: In Bayesian networks terminology this is the structure learning problem: find the graph structure (and its numerical parameterization) that explains the data at ''best".
Dans la terminologie des réseaux Bayésiens ceci est le problème d'apprentissage structurel : trouver la structure graphique (et sa paramétrisation numérique) qui explique "au mieux" les données.

tu.prop: In Bayesian networks terminology this is the structure learning problem: find the graph structure (and its numerical parameterization) that explains the data at ''best".
Our objective in this paper is not to use these algorithms (note that our ultimate goal is to develop a structure learning algorithm that is well adapted to language modeling).

tuv.seg: In BN literature there are algorithms that attempt to solve this (difficult) problem (Heckerman 1995) their performances depend on the application.
Dans la littérature BN, il existe des algorithmes qui tentent de résoudre ce problème (difficile) (Heckerman 1995) et leur performance dépend de l'application.

tu.prop: In BN literature there are algorithms that attempt to solve this (difficult) problem (Heckerman 1995) their performances depend on the application.
Our objective is rather to check whether our approach could effectively overcome classical n-gram and n-class models.

tuv.seg: Our objective in this paper is not to use these algorithms (note that our ultimate goal is to develop a structure learning algorithm that is well adapted to language modeling).

Notre but dans cet article n'est pas d'utiliser de tels algorithmes (sachant que notre objectif ultime et de développer un algorithme d'apprentissage structurel qui soit bien adapté à la modélisation du langage).

tu.prop: Our objective in this paper is not to use these algorithms (note that our ultimate goal is to develop a structure learning algorithm that is well adapted to language modeling).
Therefore we consider a rich set of graph structures that could be plausible for language modeling.

tuv.seg: Our objective is rather to check whether our approach could effectively overcome classical n-gram and n-class models.
Notre but est plutôt de vérifier que notre approche peut effectivement surpasser les modèles n-grammes et n-classes classiques.

tu.prop: Our objective is rather to check whether our approach could effectively overcome classical n-gram and n-class models.
We evaluate several DBNs in this set on a concrete application and compare them to ngram and n-class models.

tuv.seg: Therefore we consider a rich set of graph structures that could be plausible for language modeling.
Nous considérons donc un ensemble assez riche de structures graphiques qui pourraient être plausibles pour la modélisation du langage.

tu.prop: Therefore we consider a rich set of graph structures that could be plausible for language modeling.

tuv.seg: We evaluate several DBNs in this set on a concrete application and compare them to ngram and n-class models.
Ensuite, nous évaluons sur une application concrète les performances de plusieurs RBD appartenant à cet ensemble en les comparant aux n-grammes et n-classes.

tu.prop: We evaluate several DBNs in this set on a concrete application and compare them to ngram and n-class models.
In order to define a set of DBN structures plausible for language modeling we need to specify CI assertions that are linguistically informative and easy to interpret.

tuv.seg: Proposed set of structures
Ensemble de structures proposé

tu.prop: We also want n-gram and n-class models to be included in this set in order to be able to exploit their linguistic properties.

tuv.seg: In order to define a set of DBN structures plausible for language modeling we need to specify CI assertions that are linguistically informative and easy to interpret.
Pour définir un ensemble de structures de RBD plausibles pour la modélisation linguistique, nous devons spécifier des IC qui sont linguistiquement informatives et faciles à interpréter.

tu.prop: In order to define a set of DBN structures plausible for language modeling we need to specify CI assertions that are linguistically informative and easy to interpret.
To do so we start by relaxing the CI assumptions of n-class models.

tuv.seg: We also want n-gram and n-class models to be included in this set in order to be able to exploit their linguistic properties.
Nous voulons également que les modèles n-grammes et n-classes soient inclus dans cet ensemble afin de pouvoir exploiter leurs propriétés linguistiques.

tu.prop: We also want n-gram and n-class models to be included in this set in order to be able to exploit their linguistic properties.
We assume that a word does not only depend on its class but also on the classes of words in a limited past and/or future context.

tuv.seg: To do so we start by relaxing the CI assumptions of n-class models.

tu.prop: To do so we start by relaxing the CI assumptions of n-class models.
To incorporate the properties of ngrams we authorize the dependence of the word to its lexical history.

tuv.seg: We assume that a word does not only depend on its class but also on the classes of words in a limited past and/or future context.
Nous supposons qu'un mot ne dépend pas seulement de sa classe, mais aussi des classes de mots dans un contexte passé et/ou futur limité.

tu.prop: We assume that a word does not only depend on its class but also on the classes of words in a limited past and/or future context.
And finally we consider that a class may not only depend on its history but also on the current word and/or other words in a limited past and/or future context.

tuv.seg: To incorporate the properties of ngrams we authorize the dependence of the word to its lexical history.
Afin d'incorporer les propriétés des n-grammes, nous autorisons qu'un mot puisse dépendre de son historique lexical.

tu.prop: To incorporate the properties of ngrams we authorize the dependence of the word to its lexical history.
Figure 2 shows an example of a DBN in the set of structures we consider.

tuv.seg: And finally we consider that a class may not only depend on its history but also on the current word and/or other words in a limited past and/or future context.
Et enfin, nous considérons qu'une classe peut non seulement dépendre de son histoire, mais aussi du mot courant et/ou d'autres mots dans un contexte passé et/ou futur limité.

tu.prop: And finally we consider that a class may not only depend on its history but also on the current word and/or other words in a limited past and/or future context.
In this model each word depends on the previous present and future class and each class depends on previous class.

tuv.seg: Figure 2 shows an example of a DBN in the set of structures we consider.
La figure 2 montre un exemple de RBD dans l'ensemble des structures que nous considérons.

tu.prop: Figure 2 shows an example of a DBN in the set of structures we consider.
The joint probability for a specific model is expressed as:

tuv.seg: In this model each word depends on the previous present and future class and each class depends on previous class.
Dans ce modèle, chaque mot dépend de la classe précédente, présente et future et chaque classe dépend de la classe précédente.

tu.prop: In this model each word depends on the previous present and future class and each class depends on previous class.
These parameters are estimated using maximum likelihood criterion that yields :

tuv.seg: The joint probability for a specific model is expressed as:
La probabilité jointe pour un modèle spécifique est exprimée comme suit :

tu.prop: The joint probability for a specific model is expressed as:
where N xjk is the number of realizations of X t = j Π Xt = k.

tuv.seg: These parameters are estimated using maximum likelihood criterion that yields :
Ces paramètres sont estimés en utilisant le critère du maximum de vraisemblance ce qui donne :

tu.prop: These parameters are estimated using maximum likelihood criterion that yields :
The classical "smoothing" techniques are still applicable to these models.

tuv.seg: where N xjk is the number of realizations of X t = j Π Xt = k.
où N xjk est le nombre de réalisations X t = j Π Xt = k.

tu.prop: where N xjk is the number of realizations of X t = j Π Xt = k.

tuv.seg: The classical "smoothing" techniques are still applicable to these models.
Les techniques classiques de "lissage" peuvent toujours être appliqués à ces modèles.

tu.prop: The classical "smoothing" techniques are still applicable to these models.
The training and test corpus are extracted from Le monde newspaper.

tuv.seg: Experiments and evaluation
Expérimentations et évaluation

tu.prop: We use 22M words for training and a test corpus of 2M words.

tuv.seg: The training and test corpus are extracted from Le monde newspaper.
Les corpus d'apprentissage et de test sont extraits du journal Le monde.

tu.prop: The training and test corpus are extracted from Le monde newspaper.
The vocabulary consists of the most frequent 5000 words.

tuv.seg: We use 22M words for training and a test corpus of 2M words.
Nous avons utilisé 22M de mots pour l'apprentissage et 2M de mots pour le test.

tu.prop: We use 22M words for training and a test corpus of 2M words.
The training corpus has been labeled automatically by a set of 200 syntactic classes set by hand (Smaïli et al.

tuv.seg: The vocabulary consists of the most frequent 5000 words.
Le vocabulaire est constitué des 5000 mots les plus fréquents.

tu.prop: The vocabulary consists of the most frequent 5000 words.

tuv.seg: The training corpus has been labeled automatically by a set of 200 syntactic classes set by hand (Smaïli et al.
Le corpus d'apprentissage a été étiqueté automatiquement en utilisant un ensemble de 200 classes syntaxiques définies manuellement (Smaïli et al., 1999).

tu.prop: The training corpus has been labeled automatically by a set of 200 syntactic classes set by hand (Smaïli et al.
All models used in the experiments are smoothed using absolute discounting method (Ney et al.

tuv.seg: All models used in the experiments are smoothed using absolute discounting method (Ney et al.

tu.prop: All models used in the experiments are smoothed using absolute discounting method (Ney et al.
Table 1 shows perplexity results of 14 different Bayesian network language models.
The models DBN1 DBN2 and DBN3 correspond to bi-gram bi-class and triclass models respectively.

tuv.seg: Table 1 shows perplexity results of 14 different Bayesian network language models.
Le tableau 1 donne les résultats de perplexité de 14 modèles de langage correspondant à des réseau bayésiens différents.

tu.prop: Table 1 shows perplexity results of 14 different Bayesian network language models.
In order to achieve our objective to find the best model we set the bi-class model as baseline and extend it incrementally by incorporating additional lexical and/or syntactic context.

tuv.seg: The models DBN1 DBN2 and DBN3 correspond to bi-gram bi-class and triclass models respectively.
Les modèles DBN1 DBN2 et DBN3 correspondent respectivement à des modèles bigramme biclasse et triclasse.

tu.prop: The models DBN1 DBN2 and DBN3 correspond to bi-gram bi-class and triclass models respectively.
We also introduce the concept of right context of a word.

tuv.seg: In order to achieve our objective to find the best model we set the bi-class model as baseline and extend it incrementally by incorporating additional lexical and/or syntactic context.

Afin d'atteindre notre objectif de trouver le meilleur modèle, nous choisissons le modèle biclasse comme référence et nous l'étendons progressivement en incorporant un contexte lexical et/ou syntaxique supplémentaire.

tu.prop: In order to achieve our objective to find the best model we set the bi-class model as baseline and extend it incrementally by incorporating additional lexical and/or syntactic context.
DBN6 is a typical example of this case that integrates not only the left class context of a word but also its right syntactic context.

tuv.seg: We also introduce the concept of right context of a word.
Nous introduisons également le concept de contexte droit d'un mot.

tu.prop: We also introduce the concept of right context of a word.
We obtain a 16.6% improvement with respect to DBN4 that proves the importance of right context.

tuv.seg: DBN6 is a typical example of this case that integrates not only the left class context of a word but also its right syntactic context.
DBN6 est un exemple typique de ce cas qui intègre non seulement le contexte de classe gauche d'un mot, mais également son contexte syntaxique droit.

tu.prop: DBN6 is a typical example of this case that integrates not only the left class context of a word but also its right syntactic context.
It is true that linguistically this is not a surprising result.

tuv.seg: We obtain a 16.6% improvement with respect to DBN4 that proves the importance of right context.
On obtient ainsi une amélioration de 16,6 % par rapport à DBN4 qui prouve l'importance du contexte droit.

tu.prop: We obtain a 16.6% improvement with respect to DBN4 that proves the importance of right context.
On the other hand it is difficult to realize the use of right context in speech recognition but this could be achieved with a multi-pass decoding scheme.

tuv.seg: It is true that linguistically this is not a surprising result.
Il est vrai que linguistiquement parlant cela ne constitue pas une surprise.

tu.prop: It is true that linguistically this is not a surprising result.
DBN5 on the other hand shows that left context is quite important.

tuv.seg: On the other hand it is difficult to realize the use of right context in speech recognition but this could be achieved with a multi-pass decoding scheme.
En reconnaissance de la parole en revanche il est difficile d'envisager la prise en compte du contexte droit mais cela peut être rendu possible grâce à un décodage multi-passes.

tu.prop: On the other hand it is difficult to realize the use of right context in speech recognition but this could be achieved with a multi-pass decoding scheme.
That is why its removal reduces the results by 7.8%.

tuv.seg: DBN5 on the other hand shows that left context is quite important.
DBN5 d'autre part montre que le contexte de gauche est assez important.

tu.prop: DBN5 on the other hand shows that left context is quite important.
A significant perplexity reduction is observed if a word not only depends on its syntactic but also lexical context.

tuv.seg: That is why its removal reduces the results by 7.8%.
C'est pourquoi sa suppression réduit les résultats de 7,8 %.

tu.prop: That is why its removal reduces the results by 7.8%.
Indeed DBN11 yields an improvement of 24.6% with respect to DBN4.

tuv.seg: A significant perplexity reduction is observed if a word not only depends on its syntactic but also lexical context.
Une réduction considérable de la perplexité est observée lorsque on fait dépendre un mot non seulement de son contexte syntaxique mais aussi lexical.

tu.prop: A significant perplexity reduction is observed if a word not only depends on its syntactic but also lexical context.
This results confirms that lexical history is indispensable and that syntactic history provides a significant improvement.

tuv.seg: Indeed DBN11 yields an improvement of 24.6% with respect to DBN4.
En effet, DBN11 apporte une amélioration de 24,6 % par rapport à DBN4.

tu.prop: Indeed DBN11 yields an improvement of 24.6% with respect to DBN4.
Pushing forwards this strategy we achieve a model that is not only much better than the bi-class but also better than the bi-gram.

tuv.seg: This results confirms that lexical history is indispensable and that syntactic history provides a significant improvement.
Ces résultats confirment que l'historique lexical est indispensable et que l'historique syntaxique apporte une amélioration significative.

tu.prop: This results confirms that lexical history is indispensable and that syntactic history provides a significant improvement.
Indeed the model DBN14 that is shown in Fig. 3 reduces the perplexity by 57.9% with respect to DBN2 and 2.4% with respect to DBN1 (bi-gram).

tuv.seg: Pushing forwards this strategy we achieve a model that is not only much better than the bi-class but also better than the bi-gram.
En poussant cette stratégie encore plus loin nous avons conçu un modèle qui non seulement est largement meilleur que le biclasse mais qui est également meilleur que le bigramme.

tu.prop: Pushing forwards this strategy we achieve a model that is not only much better than the bi-class but also better than the bi-gram.
This new approach outperforms the classical bi-gram.

tuv.seg: Indeed the model DBN14 that is shown in Fig. 3 reduces the perplexity by 57.9% with respect to DBN2 and 2.4% with respect to DBN1 (bi-gram).
En effet, le modèle DBN14 dont la structure graphique est représentée sur la figure 3 améliore la perplexité de 57,9 % par rapport à DBN2 et de 2,4 % par rapport à DBN1 (bigramme).

tu.prop: Indeed the model DBN14 that is shown in Fig. 3 reduces the perplexity by 57.9% with respect to DBN2 and 2.4% with respect to DBN1 (bi-gram).
These results show that our approach leads indeed to a new category of language models that are able to achieve better performance than classical ones.

tuv.seg: This new approach outperforms the classical bi-gram.
Cette nouvelle approche surpasse le bigramme classique.

tu.prop: This new approach outperforms the classical bi-gram.

tuv.seg: These results show that our approach leads indeed to a new category of language models that are able to achieve better performance than classical ones.
Ces résultats montrent que notre approche conduit à une nouvelle catégorie de modèles de langage capables d'atteindre de meilleures performances que les modèles classiques.

tu.prop: These results show that our approach leads indeed to a new category of language models that are able to achieve better performance than classical ones.
We presented a new approach for language model construction based on dynamic Bayesian networks formalism.

tuv.seg: Conclusion and perspectives
Conclusion et perspectives

tu.prop: This approach has several advantages with respect to classical techniques.

tuv.seg: We presented a new approach for language model construction based on dynamic Bayesian networks formalism.
Nous avons présenté une nouvelle approche pour la construction de modèles de langage en nous appuyant sur le formalisme des réseaux Bayésiens dynamiques.

tu.prop: We presented a new approach for language model construction based on dynamic Bayesian networks formalism.
First of all it infers the best model for language from training data in such a way that the resulting model is the best explanation of the corpus and it is not constrained by a priori assumptions.

tuv.seg: This approach has several advantages with respect to classical techniques.
Cette approche présente plusieurs avantages par rapport aux techniques classiques.

tu.prop: This approach has several advantages with respect to classical techniques.
Another advantage is that all linguistic units considered in modeling are handled in one procedure.

tuv.seg: First of all it infers the best model for language from training data in such a way that the resulting model is the best explanation of the corpus and it is not constrained by a priori assumptions.

Tout d'abord, elle permet d'inférer le meilleur modèle linguistique à partir des données d'apprentissage de telle sorte que le modèle résultant soit la meilleure explication du corpus et qu'il ne soit pas contraint par des hypothèses a priori.

tu.prop: First of all it infers the best model for language from training data in such a way that the resulting model is the best explanation of the corpus and it is not constrained by a priori assumptions.
Hence the resulting models are consistent and easy to interpret.

tuv.seg: Another advantage is that all linguistic units considered in modeling are handled in one procedure.
En outre, elle incorpore en une seule et même procédure toutes les unités linguistiques considérées dans la modélisation.

tu.prop: Another advantage is that all linguistic units considered in modeling are handled in one procedure.
In this paper we tested different DBN structures in order to evaluate the potential of our approach.

tuv.seg: Hence the resulting models are consistent and easy to interpret.
Les modèles qui en résultent sont ainsi cohérents et faciles à interpréter.

tu.prop: Hence the resulting models are consistent and easy to interpret.
The results are promising and further detailed experiments are necessary to obtain better performance.

tuv.seg: In this paper we tested different DBN structures in order to evaluate the potential of our approach.
Dans cet article nous avons testé différentes structures de RBD afin d'évaluer le potentiel de notre approche.

tu.prop: In this paper we tested different DBN structures in order to evaluate the potential of our approach.
On the other hand our main objective is to develop an algorithm

tuv.seg: The results are promising and further detailed experiments are necessary to obtain better performance.
Les résultats sont prometteurs et des expériences plus approfondies sont nécessaires pour obtenir de meilleures performances.

tu.prop: The results are promising and further detailed experiments are necessary to obtain better performance.
Figure 1 : Figure 1: tri-gram and bi-class models Figure 3 : Figure 3: DBN structure that yields smaller perplexity w.r.t.

tuv.seg: On the other hand our main objective is to develop an algorithm
D'un autre côté, notre principal objectif est de développer un algorithme

tu.prop: On the other hand our main objective is to develop an algorithm

tuv.seg: Figure 1 : Figure 1: tri-gram and bi-class models Figure 3 : Figure 3: DBN structure that yields smaller perplexity w.r.t.

tu.prop: Figure 1 : Figure 1: tri-gram and bi-class models Figure 3 : Figure 3: DBN structure that yields smaller perplexity w.r.t.
-(a) (for n = 3 i.e. trigram) which is a Markov chain of order n. Class-based approaches represent the history on word classes rather than words.

tuv.seg: bi-gram.
bigramme.

tu.prop: That is if C = {l 1... l m } is the set of class labels and c T 1 = c 1...c T ∈ C

tuv.seg: -(a) (for n = 3 i.e. trigram) which is a Markov chain of order n. Class-based approaches represent the history on word classes rather than words.
-(a) (pour n = 3 soit trigramme) qui est une chaîne de Markov d'ordre n. Les approches basées sur les classes représentent l'historique par des classes de mots plutôt que par les mots.

tu.prop: -(a) (for n = 3 i.e. trigram) which is a Markov chain of order n. Class-based approaches represent the history on word classes rather than words.
T is an observed class sequence one assumes that: Table 1 : Training is performed on 11 months of "Le Monde 87".

tuv.seg: That is if C = {l 1... l m } is the set of class labels and c T 1 = c 1...c T ∈ C
C'est-à-dire que si C = {l 1... l m } est l'ensemble des étiquettes de classe et c T 1 = c 1...c T ∈ C

tu.prop: That is if C = {l 1... l m } is the set of class labels and c T 1 = c 1...c T ∈ C
Perplexity is computed using 1 month containing more than 1M words.

tuv.seg: T is an observed class sequence one assumes that: Table 1 : Training is performed on 11 months of "Le Monde 87".
T est une séquence de classe observée que l'on suppose que : Tableau 1 : L'entrainement est effectué sur 11 mois de 87 du journal Le Monde.

tu.prop: T is an observed class sequence one assumes that: Table 1 : Training is performed on 11 months of "Le Monde 87".

tuv.seg: Perplexity is computed using 1 month containing more than 1M words.
La perplexité est calculée en utilisant 1 mois contenant plus de 1M mots.
